column1: # SEMANTIC_TRAPS.tsv тАФ the Rust(bun)тЖТLOGOS divergence taxonomy (PORT.2)
# STATUS: FROZEN 2026-07-13 тАФ post-review; edits require an incident.
# The analog of a regression taxonomy: every row is code that is SYNTACTICALLY SIMILAR but
# SEMANTICALLY DIFFERENT, where a mechanical port silently breaks. Each row names a CONCRETE
# fuzz-generator focus (┬з8) тАФ the P2+ fuzz batteries target exactly these.
# SOURCE LANGUAGE (re-grounded 2026-07-13): the port source is now the RUST rewrite of bun
# (thesis "RustтЖТLOGOS"), vendored at vendor/bun/src (1516 .rs). NOT the earlier Zig 1.3.14
# pass. Rust shifts several traps vs Zig, and every bun-shape below cites a real Rust
# file:line. The LOGOS-side semantics are UNCHANGED from the prior pass (verified + reusable);
# only the bun-shape and the divergences Rust changes were rewritten.
# HOW RUST MOVES THE TRAPS (vs the old Zig grounding):
#   тАв Integer overflow тАФ Rust DEBUG builds PANIC on `+`/`*`/`-` overflow; RELEASE builds WRAP
#     (2's complement). Zig panicked in safe builds and needed `+%`/`*%` to wrap. So bun's Rust
#     hashing relies on RELEASE-wrap or EXPLICIT `wrapping_mul`/`u128`/`as` casts тАФ never plain
#     `+` in a hot wrapping loop. The trap vs LOGOS `Int` (exact, promotes to BigInt, NEVER wraps)
#     STANDS; the Rust shape to look for is `wrapping_mul` / `u64`/`u32` / `as` truncating casts.
#   тАв usize underflow тАФ Rust `len - 1` on 0 PANICS in debug, WRAPS to usize::MAX in release. bun's
#     Rust code defends with `saturating_sub(...)` PRECISELY because of this; a `- 1` with no
#     saturating guard is the landmine. LOGOS `length of xs` is signed exact `Int` тЖТ clean `-1`.
#   тАв Error handling тАФ Rust `Result<T,E>` + the `?` operator (not Zig error unions). `?` is an
#     INVISIBLE early return: `self.lexer.next()?` silently unwinds the whole parse on Err. LOGOS
#     has no `?`/`Result`; a mechanical port that drops the `?` loses the early-exit control flow.
#   тАв Strings тАФ Rust is now 3-way: `String`/`&str` (UTF-8), `&[u8]` byte parsing (the lexers work
#     on `&[u8]`), and bun's WTF-16 JS strings (`&[u16]`, `String::borrow_utf16`, lone surrogates
#     via `encode_wtf8_rune`). LOGOS `Text` = Rust `String` = UTF-8 codepoints (a FOURTH basis).
#   тАв Recursion depth тАФ bun's Rust parsers no longer use a fixed depth COUNTER; they probe the real
#     stack pointer via `StackCheck::is_safe_to_recurse()` (128K/256K headroom) and return
#     `crate::Error::StackOverflow` (тЖТ JS RangeError at the boundary). The TOML nesting test grew
#     from 25 000 to 200 000. LOGOS uses a FIXED counter (DEFAULT_MAX_AST_DEPTH=128) тАФ a different
#     mechanism, and a ported .lg parser gets NO guard for free.
# COLUMNS (tab-separated):
#   TRAP-ID     stable id (TRAP-NN), referenced by fuzz batteries + the ledger
#   class       the trap class (matches the ┬з7 named classes + discovered extras)
#   bun-shape   the bun/Rust code shape that hits it (with vendor/bun/... citation)
#   logos-shape the LOGOS construct a mechanical port would map it to (with vendor/logicaffeine/... citation)
#   divergence  WHAT breaks тАФ the concrete semantic gap. "UNVERIFIED" = claim not traced to a line.
#   fuzz-focus  the concrete ┬з8 generator focus that targets this trap
#   example     a minimal concrete case that exercises the divergence
# CITATION KEY: paths are relative to repo root. bun/... = vendor/bun/... ; lg/... = vendor/logicaffeine/...
# VERIFIED claims (traced to a cited line) are the default; anything speculative says UNVERIFIED inline.
TRAP-01
column3: bun/src/parsers/json.rs:551 `match contents[0] {` and :596 `if contents[0] == b'-'` тАФ 0-based byte access, first element at index 0 (pervasive: js_parser/p.rs:5222 `find_symbol(loc, parts[0])`)
column4: `item i of xs` / `xs[i]` тАФ 1-based; codegen lowers `item i` to `xs[(i-1) as usize]` (lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/codegen/stmt.rs bounds hints; LOGOS_QUICKGUIDE.md "item 1 тАж is the first element")
column5: Every bun `arr[0]` is the first element; the same `xs[0]` in LOGOS lowers to `xs[(0-1) as usize]` тЖТ usize underflow to a huge index тЖТ panic/OOB. A mechanical loop `for i in 0..n { arr[i] }` reads one-past and misses element 0. Rust `contents[0]` itself panics on empty (bounds check always runs, even in release), so bun's callers prove non-empty first тАФ that proof must survive the port as an explicit guard.
column6: index-boundary generation: emit accesses at 0, 1, len-1, len, len+1 against sequences of length 0/1/2/n; assert first-element = `item 1`
column7: `Let xs be [10,20,30].` тЖТ bun `contents[0]`==10 but LOGOS `xs[0]` underflows; `xs[1]`==10

column1: TRAP-02
column3: bun/src/js_parser/p.rs:5222 `let result = self.find_symbol(loc, parts[0])?;` + :6733 `strings::eql(name, &parts[0])` + p.rs:8482 `args[0] = Arg {` тАФ literal `[0]` first-element grab in parser hot paths
column4: `item 0 of xs` is a COMPILE ERROR (lg/crates/logicaffeine_language/src/parser/mod.rs:1509 ParseErrorKind::ZeroIndex; lg/crates/logicaffeine_language/src/error.rs:448 "This asks for item 0, but LOGOS indices start at 1"; NB the error lives in language/src/error.rs тАФ there is no parser/error.rs)
column5: A literal `[0]` in bun (extremely common: `parts[0]`, `args[0]`, `items[0]`) has no direct LOGOS spelling тАФ `item 0` won't compile, `xs[0]` underflows. The port MUST rewrite `[0]`тЖТ`item 1` and every `[k]`тЖТ`item (k+1)`; a missed constant off-by-one is silent for kтЙе1. Rust's `?` on the same line (TRAP-17) compounds it: the port loses BOTH the +1 and the early return.
column6: literal-zero-index census: for each ported parser, generate inputs that drive the exact `[0]`/`[const]` site and diff against oracle
column7: bun `find_symbol(loc, parts[0])` тЖТ LOGOS must read `item 1 of parts`, not `item 0`

column1: TRAP-03
column3: bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:66-70 `mum`: `let mut r = (a as u128) * (b as u128); r = (r >> 64) ^ r; r as u64` + :573 `let x = (*a as u128).wrapping_mul(*b as u128); *a = x as u64` тАФ deliberate 128тЖТ64 truncating hash arithmetic; RELEASE wrap / explicit `wrapping_mul`
column4: Mapping the hash accumulator to LOGOS `Int` (lg/crates/logicaffeine_data/src/types.rs:29 `pub type Int = i64;`), whose runtime arithmetic is EXACT and PROMOTES to BigInt on i64 overflow (lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/semantics/arith.rs:443-446 "on i64 overflow it promotes to BigInt instead of wrapping")
column5: Rust wyhash truncates in тДд/2тБ╢тБ┤ via `as u64` / `wrapping_mul`; LOGOS `Int` does NOT wrap тАФ it silently grows to arbitrary precision, so the digest diverges the instant any intermediate would have overflowed 2тБ╢тБ┤. The CORRECT LOGOS target is a `Word64` (lg/crates/logicaffeine_base/src/word.rs:1 "the ring тДд/2с╡ПтДд тАж total and wrapping: Word32::MAX.add(ONE)==ZERO"). Port must use Word*, never Int, for any wrapping algorithm. NOTE Rust-specific: a plain `*` here would PANIC in debug тАФ bun uses `wrapping_mul`/`u128` on purpose; that is the tell.
column6: near-MAX/MIN arithmetic: feed hash/parse inputs whose accumulators cross 2тБ╢тБ┤/2┬│┬▓; differential Wyhash::hash(bytes) LOGOS-Word64 vs bun (test vectors at wyhash/lib.rs:998); assert bit-exact
column7: `Wyhash::hash(0,"abc")` = 0x32ddтАж (wyhash/lib.rs:1011): every `mum` truncates to u64; LOGOS-Int promotes to BigInt тЖТ wrong digest

column1: TRAP-04
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml/lexer.rs:449-451 `let mut number: u32 = 0; for &c in text { number = number * 10 + u32::from(c - b'0'); }` тАФ digit accumulation into a fixed-width u32 (fast integer path)
column4: LOGOS numeric literal parse into `Int` (arith.rs:443 exact-promote) or into `Word32` (word.rs wrapping)
column5: If ported to Int: no overflow at all (grows to BigInt) тАФ an integer that bun would wrap/mis-parse as u32-overflow is accepted at full precision; if ported to Word32: wraps silently where Rust `number * 10` PANICS in a DEBUG build on overflow (RELEASE wraps). Three-way outcome from one site: debug-panic / release-wrap / LOGOS-BigInt. The port must choose the width AND overflow policy the grammar actually intends. bun guards this path with `self.end - self.start < 10` (lexer.rs:447) so u32 can't overflow тАФ that тЙд9-digit guard is invisible-to-port and must be carried.
column6: near-MAX/MIN + overflow-boundary literals: generate integer literals at u32/u64/i64 max┬▒1 and 2^53┬▒1 for each numeric parser; diff parse-or-reject; hit the `< 10` digit guard exactly
column7: `4294967296` (2┬│┬▓, 10 digits): bun takes the double path (guard fails) тЖТ 4294967296.0; a naive port to the u32 loop wraps to 0

column1: TRAP-05
column3: bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:311 `let aligned_len = input.len() - (input.len() % 32);` + :515 `let quarter = (input.len() >> 3) << 2;` and any `a / b` / `a % b` on usize
column4: LOGOS `Divide`/modulo returns Result Err on b==0 (lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/semantics/arith.rs:850,865,873 `return Err("Division by zero")`); `/` truncates toward zero, `//` floors (resolve_division)
column5: Rust `/` and `%` on a zero divisor PANIC ("attempt to divide by zero") in BOTH debug and release тАФ a hard, uncatchable abort; LOGOS raises a CATCHABLE error, never a panic. Behavior at the boundary i64::MIN/-1 also differs: Rust `i64::MIN / -1` panics with overflow, LOGOS promotes to BigInt 2тБ╢┬│ (arith.rs:875-876). Truncate-vs-floor on negatives is a separate silent gap: LOGOS `/`==trunc, `//`==floor тАФ pick the wrong operator and negatives diverge.
column6: div-by-zero + negative-div + MIN/-1: generate operand pairs {0 divisor, negative/negative, i64::MIN ├╖ -1}; diff result-or-error
column7: `-7 / 2`: LOGOS `/`тЖТ-3 (trunc), `//`тЖТ-4 (floor); i64::MIN/-1: Rust panics, LOGOSтЖТBigInt 9223372036854775808

column1: TRAP-06
column3: bun/src/parsers/json.rs:542 `unescaped[n - 2] == b'\\' && unescaped[n - 1] == quote` (guarded by `n >= 3`) тАФ and the DEFENSIVE `saturating_sub` shapes bun uses BECAUSE of underflow: toml/lexer.rs:918 `(iter.i as usize + width2 as usize).saturating_sub(2)`, :993/:1010 `(start + iter.i as usize).saturating_sub(width2 as usize)`
column4: LOGOS `length of xs` yields `Int` (arith exact); `length of xs - 1` computes in signed exact arithmetic тЖТ `-1`, no wrap
column5: Rust `usize` `len - 1` with `len==0` PANICS in debug ("attempt to subtract with overflow") and WRAPS to 18446744073709551615 in release тАФ which is why bun litters the escape-decode paths with `saturating_sub`; a bare `- 1` that bun left unguarded is a real landmine. LOGOS `Int` gives a clean `-1`, which then either (a) becomes `item 0`тЖТZeroIndex compile err, or (b) `item -1`тЖТunderflow at `(тИТ1тИТ1) as usize`. The `saturating_sub` clamp-to-0 has NO LOGOS equivalent unless ported explicitly тАФ LOGOS just yields the true negative. Empty-input paths are the trigger.
column6: empty-and-length-1 inputs: for every `len-1`/`saturating_sub` port site, drive length 0 and 1; assert graceful (matches oracle's clamped-to-0, not a huge index and not a raw negative index)
column7: Decode `""` at toml/lexer.rs:918: bun clamps `.saturating_sub(2)`тЖТ0; a LOGOS port of `x - 2` yields -2 тЖТ different offset / index error

column1: TRAP-07
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml/lexer.rs:73 `pub string_literal_slice: &'a [u8]` тАФ the lexers parse `&[u8]` BYTES; JS strings are WTF-16 via bun/src/bun_core/string/mod.rs:191 `pub fn borrow_utf16(s: &[u16]) -> Self` (16-bit code UNITS, may hold lone surrogates)
column4: LOGOS `Text` = Rust `String` = UTF-8 (lg/crates/logicaffeine_data/src/types.rs:33 `pub type Text = String;`), Text indexing is by Unicode SCALAR (lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/semantics/collections.rs:49 `chars().count()`; :119 `chars().nth(idx)`)
column5: FOUR incompatible indexing bases in the Rust source: bun's LEXERS index `&[u8]` by BYTE, bun's JS String is WTF-16 code UNITS (`&[u16]`), bun's `bun_core::String` also carries a LATIN-1 variant (mod.rs:228 `clone_latin1`, bytes 128-255 are single chars тЙа UTF-8), and LOGOS `Text` indexes by CODEPOINT. `item i of text` counts CODEPOINTS; a mechanical port of a byte offset, a UTF-16 code-unit offset, or a Latin-1 byte lands on the wrong character for any non-ASCII/astral input. Worse: Rust `String` CANNOT hold a lone surrogate, so any bun WTF-16 value with an unpaired surrogate is unrepresentable in LOGOS Text and must use bytes/Word16.
column6: surrogate-pairs + multibyte + latin1-high-byte + lone-surrogates: generate strings with astral chars (ЁЯША U+1F600), 2-3 byte UTF-8, Latin-1 bytes 0x80-0xFF, and unpaired D800..DFFF; diff length + nth-char + roundtrip
column7: `"aЁЯШАb"`: bun byte-len=6, JS UTF-16 length=4, LOGOS `length`=3 (codepoints); `item 2` is ЁЯША in LOGOS, a lead byte in the lexer's &[u8] view

column1: TRAP-08
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml/lexer.rs:1173 `let len = strings::encode_wtf8_rune(&mut part, iter.c as u32); buf.extend_from_slice(&part[0..len]);` тАФ a decoded codepoint > U+FFFF is emitted as WTF-8 (the surrogate-pair carrier for astral chars in string cooking)
column4: LOGOS has no code-unit surrogate concept; astral chars are single `Char`/scalar values (collections.rs chars()-based). To reproduce WTF-16 output the port must operate on `Word16` sequences, not Text.
column5: The surrogate-splitting is invisible in a Text-based port: LOGOS treats ЁЯША as one scalar; JS `.length` (a UTF-16 code-unit count) sees TWO. Any routine whose OUTPUT is measured in UTF-16 units (JS `.length`, source-map columns, the `\uXXXX` escape emitter at lexer.rs:1031-1128 which accumulates a fixed-width value then `iter.c = value as CodePoint`) will be off by exactly the astral count. A LOGOS `length of text` (codepoints) silently under-reports for astral text. bun deliberately widens the `\u{тАж}` accumulator to `i64` (lexer.rs:1034 "so we don't risk integer overflows when people do weird things") and saturates (:1068 `saturating_mul(16)`) тАФ that overflow-safety is invisible-to-port.
column6: astral-plane density: strings with varying counts of >U+FFFF chars; assert JS-semantics length (UTF-16 units) and source-map column parity; drive `\u{10FFFF}` and `\u{110000}` (out-of-range at lexer.rs:1078)
column7: `"ЁЭХП".length` (JS)==2 (one surrogate pair); LOGOS `length`==1 тЖТ source-map column off by 1

column1: TRAP-09
column3: bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:436 `self.buf[self.buf_len..self.buf_len + input.len()].copy_from_slice(input);` + :344 `self.buf[..].copy_from_slice(&b[0..off])` тАФ `&mut self` mutates the caller-owned streaming buffer in place; and every `&mut self` method (Wyhash::update, Lexer::next) edits shared state through a reference
column4: LOGOS collections/structs are VALUE-SEMANTIC (copy-on-write): assignment COPIES (lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/semantics/collections.rs:394-395 "Value semantics is the DEFAULT (all four tiers)")
column5: Rust mutates through `&mut T`: a `&mut self` method edits the caller's buffer; two `&mut` borrows can't coexist but a stored struct + its holder do (the `Lexer` borrows `&'a Source`, mutates `self.current`). In LOGOS, once a collection is bound to a second name (or passed/stored), a mutation COW-copies and the other holder is FROZEN. A ported algorithm relying on "mutate a builder the caller also holds" silently splits into two independent copies тАФ the caller never sees the writes. Rust's own borrow checker forbids the aliased-&mut shape, so the naive port is where the divergence hides.
column6: mutate-after-copy / aliased-builder: generate programs that bind ys=xs, store xs in a struct, then Push to xs; assert the alias is UNCHANGED (COW) and matches oracle only where bun also copies
column7: `Let ys be xs. Push 99 to xs.` тЖТ LOGOS: `ys` unchanged (COW); a port assuming shared `&mut` mutation would expect `ys` to grow too

column1: TRAP-10
column3: bun/src/js_parser/repl_transforms.rs:512 `parts[0].stmts = bun_ast::StoreSlice::new_mut(final_slice);` тАФ in-place overwrite of a live slot in shared arena storage; bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:481 `scratch[0..rem].copy_from_slice(&self.buf[48 - rem..48])` in-place scratch fill
column4: LOGOS list write `Set item i of xs to v` / SetIndex COW-copies if xs is shared (collections.rs value-semantics)
column5: Rust `slice[i] = v` / `copy_from_slice` assume the destination is THE live buffer everyone reads. A LOGOS SetIndex on a shared list triggers copy-on-write, so mutations land on a private copy while other holders keep the old data. Divergence is silent and order-dependent (first-write-wins vs last-write-visible). Note the Rust index write ALSO carries TRAP-01 (0-based) and TRAP-06 (usize) тАФ one line, three traps.
column6: shared-buffer in-place write: fuzz programs that alias a list into тЙе2 holders then index-write; diff every holder's post-state
column7: `Let a be xs. Set item 1 of xs to 99. Show item 1 of a.` тЖТ LOGOS shows original (COW); a `slice[0] = 99` port expects 99

column1: TRAP-11
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml/lexer.rs:60-73 `pub source: &'a bun_ast::Source тАж pub string_literal_slice: &'a [u8]` (comment: "borrow `&'a [u8]` from `source.contents` without a self-referential struct") + toml/lexer.rs:1 `use bun_alloc::Arena; // bumpalo::Bump re-export` тАФ AST/lexer nodes hold `&'a [u8]` slices BORROWED from the source buffer / bumpalo arena
column4: LOGOS AST is arena-allocated with lifetimes (lg/crates/logicaffeine_base/src/arena.rs "bump allocation тАж references remain valid throughout parsing тАж zero-copy"); runtime VALUES are owned/Rc-shared, not borrows into the source
column5: bun's Rust parser nodes are cheap `&'a` borrows tied to the source buffer's lifetime тАФ Rust's borrow checker ENFORCES they can't dangle (the port SHAPE, borrow vs own, is caught at compile time, not a runtime divergence). But a port that models "AST node = zero-copy `&'a [u8]` view" as "LOGOS runtime Text = owned Rc copy" changes allocation/aliasing cost AND identity (bun: pointer-equal slices into one buffer; LOGOS: distinct owned copies). And storing a LOGOS borrow past the arena's `'a` scope is a LOGOS compile error, matching Rust тАФ so this trap fails LOUD (build error), never silent.
column6: AUDIT (compile-time, NOT a corpus fuzzer): a per-node-type lifetime-shape audit тАФ assert each ported node either borrows source (arena `'a`) or owns (Rc) matching bun's ownership. The only runtime-fuzzable slice = large-source retention (does freeing the input buffer invalidate the output?); the rest is a manual ┬з8-author checklist item, not a generator.
column7: Lexer stores `string_literal_slice: &'a [u8]` into `source.contents`; naive LOGOS port stores owned Text тЖТ correct but O(n) copies + loses pointer identity; storing a borrow past arena scope тЖТ LOGOS compile error

column1: TRAP-12
column3: bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:34 `debug_assert!(data.len() >= usize::from(BYTES));` + :184 `debug_assert!(b.len() == 32)` + :219 `debug_assert!(b.len() < 32)` тАФ `debug_assert!` STRIPPED in release; also lexer.rs:1169 `u8::try_from(iter.c).expect("int cast")` (a survives-release check)
column4: LOGOS `Assert that P.` тЖТ `debug_assert!` (STRIPPED in release; lg/crates/logicaffeine_compile/src/codegen/stmt.rs:2281-2283, the `Stmt::Assert` arm); `Require that P.` тЖТ `assert!` (SURVIVES release; the `Stmt::RuntimeAssert{hard}` arm, stmt.rs:2296-2300 "`Require` тЖТ `assert!` (enforced, survives); `Assert` тЖТ `debug_assert!` (a development check, stripped)")
column5: If a bun `debug_assert!`'s condition (or a ported `Assert`'s) contains a SIDE EFFECT тАФ a mutation, a counter bump, an effecting call тАФ that effect VANISHES in release, forking debug-vs-release behavior. Mapping trap: a bun `debug_assert!` (debug-only invariant) must map to LOGOS `Assert` (also stripped), NOT `Require` (always runs); mapping it to `Require` makes it fire in production, mapping a load-bearing `assert!`/`.expect()` to `Assert` makes a real check disappear. Choose per-site by whether bun used `debug_assert!` (тЖТAssert) or `assert!`/`.expect()`/`.unwrap()` (тЖТRequire). The wyhash `debug_assert!`s are SAFETY preconditions for the following `unsafe` unaligned loads тАФ dropping them in a port that keeps the unsafe read is UB.
column6: AUDIT via a build-mode DIFFERENTIAL HARNESS (not a corpus fuzzer): compile each ported `Assert that f(x)` in debug AND release, run both, and diff тАФ debugтЙбrelease is the invariant (assert conditions must be pure/side-effect-free). The generator only supplies the effect-bearing `f`; the trap is caught by the two-build diff, not by input mutation.
column7: `Assert that (Push 1 to log) is true.` тЖТ release strips it, log stays empty; debug pushes. Behavior forks on build mode.

column1: TRAP-13
column3: bun/src/wyhash/lib.rs:33 `fn read_bytes<const BYTES: u8>(data: &[u8])` (comment: "Rust cannot mint an integer type from a const generic; dispatch on the only values used") + lexer.rs:849 `fn decode_escape_sequences<const ALLOW_MULTILINE: bool>` тАФ compile-time monomorphized width/flag specialization
column4: LOGOS has no user const-generics; compile-time specialization maps to monomorphized generics / const folding by the optimizer, and const flags map to build features or plain runtime `if` (no cited const-generic keyword in LOGOS тАФ UNVERIFIED that an exact analog exists)
column5: Rust `const BYTES`/`const ALLOW_MULTILINE` do two jobs a port conflates: (a) BRANCH specialization (`decode_escape_sequences::<true>` vs `::<false>` are two distinct compiled functions тАФ the `if ALLOW_MULTILINE` at lexer.rs:991 is eliminated per instantiation), and (b) WIDTH specialization (`read_bytes::<4>` bakes the 4-byte load). A port that turns a monomorphized const-flag into a single runtime `if` keeps both branches in one function (correctness-neutral but a perf/size regression, and the eliminated branch now executes). Const-width types become runtime-checked.
column6: AUDIT (compile-time site census, NOT a corpus fuzzer): enumerate each `<const тАж>` / monomorphized site and assert both `::<true>` and `::<false>` instantiations are reachable and behavior-matched, and that release output carries no debug-only branch artifact. A build-mode differential harness can confirm the branch parity, but there is no input-corpus generator here тАФ it is a per-site checklist for the ┬з8 author.
column7: `decode_escape_sequences::<true>` (multiline) errors on `\x` (lexer.rs:991); `::<false>` allows it тАФ a runtime-`if` port must reproduce BOTH per call site

column1: TRAP-14
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml.rs:318 `if !self.stack_check.is_safe_to_recurse() { return Err(crate::Error::StackOverflow); }` + json.rs:969/1008 same guard; the guard PROBES THE STACK POINTER (bun/src/bun_core/util.rs:2350 `is_safe_to_recurse`: `frame_address().saturating_sub(cached_stack_end) > 128K/256K`), NOT a fixed counter
column4: LOGOS parser has a FIXED AST-depth counter (lg/crates/logicaffeine_language/src/ast_depth.rs:45 `DEFAULT_MAX_AST_DEPTH = 128`; parser/mod.rs:2114 `enter_recursion` тЖТ Err AstTooDeep at depth limit+1) тАФ but that limit is the LANGUAGE parser's, NOT a limit a ported TOML/JSON `.lg` parser gets for free
column5: MECHANISM MISMATCH (this is the big Rust change vs the Zig version): bun's Rust parsers guard by REMAINING STACK SPACE (a runtime probe, so the effective depth depends on frame size and the 18 MB main-thread stack), returning a Rust `Err(StackOverflow)` that the JS boundary turns into a catchable `RangeError`. The nesting test now needs depth 200_000 (bun/test/js/bun/resolve/toml/toml.test.js:110, "Previously 25_000"), not 25k. A ported LOGOS JSON/TOML parser is ordinary user code with NO automatic per-parser guard тАФ deep input recurses the tree-walker/VM until the HOST stack overflows and the process ABORTS (ast_depth.rs "overflows their stacks and aborts the process"), which is NOT a catchable error. Every ported recursive parser MUST re-implement an explicit depth/stack guard that yields a catchable error; forgetting it turns a spec-required RangeError into a crash. And a FIXED counter (LOGOS's 128) rejects at a very different, input-independent depth than bun's stack probe.
column6: deep-nesting generators: emit `{ b = { b = тАж 1 тАж } }`-style towers at depths {100, 1k, 25k, 200k, 500k} for each recursive parser; assert a CATCHABLE RangeError (not a crash, not silent success) and match bun's throw/accept boundary
column7: `Bun.TOML.parse("a = " + "{ b = ".repeat(200000) + "1" + " }".repeat(200000))` MUST throw RangeError (toml.test.js:113); a guard-less LOGOS port stack-overflows and aborts

column1: TRAP-15
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml/lexer.rs:451 `u32::from(c - b'0')` (digit) + :922 `let mut value: i64 = (c2 - '0' as CodePoint) as i64` (octal) + :1274 `matches!(code_point as u32 as u8 as char, '0'..='9' | 'a'..='z' | тАж)` тАФ byte/char arithmetic and `as u8` range compares
column4: LOGOS `Char`/`Text` comparisons operate on Unicode scalars, not raw bytes; a byte `u8` in [0..255] maps to a scalar or must be a `Word8`
column5: Rust freely does byte arithmetic (`c - b'0'`, `code_point as u32 as u8 as char`, ASCII range compares) on `u8`/`CodePoint`; LOGOS Text is codepoints and `c - '0'` on a Char is not the same op. Rust-specific: `c - b'0'` where `c < b'0'` PANICS in debug (u8 subtract overflow) тАФ bun's callers gate on the digit range first, an invisible-to-port guard. If the port keeps bytes as `Word8`, `<`/`>` are ring compares (fine for ASCII); if it decodes to Char/Text, byte tricks (`- '0'`, `as u8 as char`) don't apply to multibyte and the ASCII fast-paths silently mis-handle bytes тЙе 0x80. Note lexer.rs:1282 explicitly bounds `code_point` to `0..=127` "required for the byte cast above to be sound" тАФ that soundness guard must port.
column6: ascii-boundary + high-byte: generate inputs mixing ASCII digits/letters with bytes 0x80-0xFF and multibyte UTF-8; diff digit/letter classification and the `as u8 as char` fast path
column7: `c - b'0'` on a byte < '0' (e.g. 0x20 space): bun's caller gated it; an ungated LOGOS/Word8 port wraps, a Char port sees a scalar and the trick is meaningless

column1: TRAP-16
column3: bun/src/parsers/json.rs:1466 `let Data::ENumber(n) = &a.items.last().unwrap().data` and :2070 `doc.len() - 1` тАФ "last element" via `.last().unwrap()` / `len-1` with the emptiness assumed away
column4: LOGOS `item (length of xs) of xs` for last; on empty xs, `length`==0 тЖТ `item 0` compile-error/underflow (ties TRAP-01/06)
column5: The idiom "last = arr[len-1]" / `.last().unwrap()` assumes non-empty: Rust `.last().unwrap()` PANICS on empty (uncatchable), `len - 1` on 0 underflows (TRAP-06). In LOGOS the same expression on an empty collection is a guaranteed ZeroIndex/underflow, so ports of "peek last / pop / trim trailing" must add an explicit emptiness guard bun left implicit (or expressed as `.unwrap()`, i.e. Require-not-Assert semantics). Silent on non-empty, hard-fail on empty on BOTH sides тАФ but with different failure kinds (Rust panic vs LOGOS compile/underflow error).
column6: empty-and-singleton corpus: for every "last element"/pop/peek/`.last()`/`.unwrap()` port, drive length 0 and 1; assert guarded behavior matches oracle (both non-silent)
column7: `item (length of xs) of xs` with `xs` empty тЖТ `item 0` тЖТ ZeroIndex; bun `items.last().unwrap()` panics тАФ both must be caught, neither is the default

column1: TRAP-17
column3: bun/src/parsers/toml.rs:321 `self.parse_value_inner()` reached only via :319 `return Err(тАж)` on the guard, and pervasive `self.lexer.next()?` (toml/lexer.rs:341,476) / `self.expect(token)?` (lexer.rs:471-477) / `self.find_symbol(loc, parts[0])?` (p.rs:5222) тАФ the `?` operator is an INVISIBLE early return on `Err`
column4: LOGOS has no `?`/`Result`; error propagation is explicit (`Require that тАж` aborts, or a returned value / early `Give back`). No cited `?`-equivalent operator in LOGOS (UNVERIFIED that a one-char analog exists)
column5: Rust's `?` desugars `expr?` to `match expr { Ok(v)=>v, Err(e)=>return Err(e.into()) }` тАФ a HIDDEN control-flow exit on every `?`-suffixed call. A mechanical port that transcribes `self.lexer.next()?` as a plain `next()` call DROPS the early return: on a lex error the ported parser keeps going with stale/garbage state instead of unwinding, turning a clean SyntaxError into corrupted output or a later unrelated crash. Every `?` is a branch that must become explicit LOGOS error handling; `?` also runs `.into()` on the error (type conversion between crate `Error` enums) that a port must reproduce. This is Rust-specific: Zig error unions had `try` which is at least visually present; `?` is a single trailing char easy to miss.
column6: question-mark-census: for each ported parser fn, enumerate every `?` site, feed an input that makes THAT call Err (bad token, EOF mid-literal, unterminated string), and assert the ported LOGOS parser exits at the same point with the same error тАФ not later, not never
column7: Unterminated TOML string: bun `self.add_default_error(тАж)?` (lexer.rs:643) unwinds immediately; a port that drops the `?` continues lexing past EOF into a second, misleading error

column1: TRAP-18
column3: bun/src/semver/Version.rs:419-459 `pub fn order_without_tag(lhs, rhs) -> Ordering` (a Less/Greater/Equal ladder over major/minor/patch/pre), consumed by `match order { Ordering::Equal => тАж, Ordering::Greater => тАж, Ordering::Less => тАж }` at bun/src/semver/SemverRange.rs:256 and by the comparator `sort_gt` (Version.rs:106 `order_fn(тАж) == Ordering::Greater`)
column4: LOGOS has `is less than`/`is greater than`/`is equal to` (lg/crates/logicaffeine_tests/tests/codegen_hotpath_locks.rs:85, audit_codegen.rs:33, correctness_is_equality.rs:67) but NO built-in 3-way compare / `Ordering` / `cmp` (exhaustive corpus + QUICKGUIDE search: none). Model it as a user enum `## An Ordering is one of: A Less. An Equal. A Greater.` (payload-free enum, e2e_codegen_enums.rs:17 form) returned explicitly and dispatched with `Inspect`/`When` (correctness_inspect.rs:21)
column5: Rust returns a single `Ordering` value that a `match` fans out three ways in ONE place; a port that reaches for pairwise `is less than`/`is greater than` booleans re-evaluates the whole major/minor/patch/pre ladder at every comparison site AND risks an inconsistent partial order (e.g. an `a<b` test and a separate `a==b` test that disagree on the pre-release tie-break). The correct port computes the `Ordering` ONCE (one `## To compareVersion (a, b) -> Ordering`) and `Inspect`s it тАФ mirroring bun's single `order_without_tag` reused by `order`, `satisfies`, and `sort_gt`. The total-order axioms (antisymmetry, transitivity) that bun's ladder guarantees must hold for the ported enum, or version selection/sort corrupts.
column6: total-order axioms over version triples: generate random (major, minor, patch, pre) triples and assert the ported `compareVersion` is a TOTAL ORDER тАФ antisymmetric (cmp(a,b)==Greater тЯ║ cmp(b,a)==Less), transitive (aтЙдb тИз bтЙдc тЗТ aтЙдc), and Equal-reflexive тАФ then differential-diff each verdict vs bun `Version::order_without_tag`
column7: `1.2.0` vs `1.2.0-rc.1`: bun ladder тЖТ Greater (a release outranks its pre-release, Version.rs:439-451); a naive pairwise port that forgets the `has_pre()` tie-break returns Equal тЖТ wrong install pick

column1: TRAP-19
column3: bun/src/glob/matcher.rs:188 `'main_loop: while тАж` with `continue 'main_loop;` (:227,256,350), `break 'fallthrough;`/`break 'to_else;` (:268,270,352), and BREAK-WITH-VALUE `break 'blk high;`/`break 'brk cp;` (:330,648); plus plain `continue;` (:422) тАФ labeled multi-level loop control is structural to the glob matcher
column4: LOGOS `Break.` exits the INNERMOST loop ONLY (lg/crates/logicaffeine_language/src/ast/stmt.rs:282 "exits the innermost while loop"; LOGOS_QUICKGUIDE.md:150 "innermost loop"); there is NO `Continue` statement (token.rs:48 `Continue` is a Logic-mode PresupKind, not loop control тАФ no Stmt::Continue in ast/stmt.rs), NO labeled break, NO break-with-value
column5: A mechanical port of `continue 'main_loop` / `break 'label` / `break 'blk value` has no LOGOS keyword: (a) plain `continue` тЖТ guard the loop's REST with an `If` (skip the tail when the continue-condition holds); (b) labeled `continue`/`break` targeting an OUTER loop тЖТ thread a mutable `done`/`skip` flag out through each nesting level and re-check it after the inner loop; (c) `break 'blk value` тЖТ assign the value to a var first, THEN `Break`. Silent-divergence risk: a `break 'main_loop` transcribed as `Break.` exits only the inner loop and the outer keeps running тЖТ the matcher accepts/rejects the wrong path. The whole glob matcher is a STRUCTURAL REWRITE (flag-threading), not a transliteration.
column6: label-target census + control-flow differential: for each ported loop with a labeled `break`/`continue`, drive an input that triggers THAT jump and assert the ported control flow lands where bun's did (same next iteration / same loop exited); glob-match corpus of `*`/`**`/`?`/`[тАж]`/`{тАж}` patterns ├Ч paths, diff accept/reject vs bun
column7: bun `break 'main_loop` at glob/matcher.rs:227-region exits the OUTER while; a `Break.` port exits only the inner block тЖТ outer loop over-runs, `**` globstar matches one segment too many

column1: TRAP-20
column3: bun/src/parsers/json.rs:537 `matches!(contents[1], b'"' | b'\'')` + json.rs:794 `matches!(contents[p], b']' | b',')` + ini/lib.rs:121 `matches!(self, ConfigOpt::_Auth | ConfigOpt::_Password)`; guarded/OR-of-range match arms json.rs:555 `b'-' | b'.' if leads_a_number(contents) =>`, js_parser/lexer.rs:688 `0x30..=0x37 =>` and :1289 `0x30..=0x39 | 0x61..=0x66 | 0x41..=0x46 =>`
column4: `matches!`/OR-pattern тЖТ an `Inspect`/`If`-chain: LOGOS `Inspect` `When Variant (bindings):` parses ONE variant per arm (parser/mod.rs:5756) тАФ there is NO `When a or b:` OR-arm in the corpus or QUICKGUIDE (UNVERIFIED/unsupported). Byte/value ORs use the logical `a or b` operator in an `If` (QUICKGUIDE:55) or `is X or is Y`; contiguous ranges use `is between lo and hi` (inclusive, correctness_predicates.rs:97, QUICKGUIDE:53); a Rust match GUARD (`pat if cond`) becomes an `If` nested inside the `When` arm
column5: Three conflations in one construct: (a) `matches!(x, A | B)` is a BOOLEAN test, not a match statement тАФ port it to `x is A or x is B` inside an `If`, NOT an `Inspect` (an `Inspect` with an empty `Otherwise` silently no-ops). (b) An enum OR-arm `When A | B:` has no LOGOS spelling тАФ you must write TWO `When` arms with the same body (duplication a mechanical port won't produce). (c) A range `0x30..=0x37` maps to `is between 48 and 55` (remember 1-based/byte-value: the RANGE VALUES don't shift, only Seq INDICES do тАФ ┬з3/TRAP-01). Dropping the `if cond` guard (json.rs:555) or collapsing two range arms silently mis-classifies exactly the boundary bytes.
column6: or-pattern + range-boundary parity: for each ported `matches!`/OR/range arm, drive inputs at every alternative AND every range endpoint (lo-1, lo, hi, hi+1) and diff the classification vs bun; assert an OR-arm that a port split into two `When`s covers BOTH variants
column7: `0x30..=0x37` (octal digits '0'..'7'): port to `is between 48 and 55` тАФ feeding byte 0x38 ('8') must FALL THROUGH (not match), catching an off-by-one range port; `matches!(c, b'"' | b'\'')` тЖТ `c is 34 or c is 39`, NOT an Inspect
